label: Uneasy calm
summary:
The weekend opened with the Iran war edging toward an off-ramp. President Trump said the U.S. and Iran could come to terms 'maybe over the weekend in Europe,' pledged to send Vice President JD Vance to sign, and said the Strait of Hormuz would 'officially open as soon as we sign'; Pakistan, mediating, said a 'final, agreed upon text' of the 14-point deal had been reached, with Iran committing indefinitely never to develop nuclear weapons and to reopen the strait within 30 days. The caveats were heavy — Iran's foreign ministry said nothing was finalized, the U.S. military again downed Iranian drones near the strait, and the two sides offered conflicting accounts — so this is hope, not yet peace, ahead of next week's G7 summit in the French Alps. Markets had already read the de-escalation as relief: Friday's session closed mixed but steady (Dow +0.57%, S&P 500 roughly flat, Nasdaq -0.30%) with oil easing, after SpaceX's record Nasdaq debut.

The rest of the ledger stayed genuinely mixed. The recovery from the magnitude-7.8 Mindanao earthquake — the Philippines' strongest in decades — ground on, with the toll past 60 dead, more than 1,200 injured and over 340,000 people affected. Eastern DR Congo's untreatable Bundibugyo-Ebola outbreak and famine across Sudan's besieged El Fasher and Darfur continued to grind, Russia's war on Ukraine pressed on, and South Korea sentenced ousted president Yoon Suk Yeol to 30 years for a 2024 drone provocation against the North — a rule-of-law reckoning wrapped in lingering political turmoil. Against the strain, brighter notes accumulated: the World Cup's U.S. return drew a 4-1 American rout of Paraguay and Canada's first-ever World Cup point, a France-sized marine reserve was created around French Polynesia, mangrove forests posted their first net gain in 40 years, solar out-generated coal in the U.S. for the first time in a month, and researchers reported a blood test that flags lung-cancer risk years early. (Scored on the day's facts: a near-final Iran deal and steady markets lift the day to the upper edge of the 'mixed' band, but a deadly quake aftermath, famine and an unsigned, contested ceasefire keep it short of 'good.')

drivers.headline: Trump says a U.S.-Iran deal to end the war could be signed 'over the weekend in Europe,' with VP Vance to sign and the Strait of Hormuz to reopen; Pakistan says a 'final, agreed upon text' has been reached

Iran's foreign ministry says nothing has been finalized and the U.S. downs more Iranian drones near the Strait of Hormuz, as the two sides give conflicting accounts of the tentative peace deal ahead of the G7 summit
U.S. stocks close mixed but steady (Dow +0.57%, S&P 500 roughly flat, Nasdaq -0.30%) as oil eases on Iran de-escalation hopes and SpaceX makes a record Nasdaq debut
Recovery from the magnitude-7.8 Mindanao earthquake, the Philippines' strongest in decades, grinds on with more than 60 dead, over 1,200 injured and some 346,000 people affected
Famine persists across Sudan's besieged El Fasher and North Darfur, leaving people trapped in the city facing starvation in the world's worst hunger crisis
Eastern DR Congo's untreatable Bundibugyo-Ebola outbreak collides with conflict and hunger, the WHO warns, with cross-border spread to Uganda and no licensed vaccine or treatment
South Korea sentences ousted president Yoon Suk Yeol and his former defense chief to 30 years for ordering a 2024 drone incursion over Pyongyang to manufacture a pretext for martial law
The World Cup returns to U.S. soil for the first time in 32 years as the U.S. routs Paraguay 4-1 and Canada earns its first-ever World Cup point in a 1-1 draw with Bosnia and Herzegovina
A France-sized marine reserve of about 200,000 square miles is created around French Polynesia's Austral and Marquesas islands, barring mining, trawling and industrial fishing
Mangrove forests post their first net gain in 40 years as conservation reverses decades of loss, in what researchers call a global turning point
U.S. solar out-generates coal for the first time in a single month, supplying 12.8% of electricity in May against 12.2% from coal
Researchers identify 14 blood proteins that can predict lung-cancer risk years in advance, drawn from a study of more than 48,000 UK participants

sources_consulted: Wikipedia (2026 Iran war ceasefire / 2026 Mindanao earthquake / Twelve-Day War ceasefire / El Fasher massacre / 2026 FIFA World Cup)
